directive.title: FS-AUTH-003: /api/internal/audit Endpoint for Access Denial Log Ingestion

auditor.summary: Build passes all acceptance criteria and resolves all prior findings. The timing-safe comparison now uses fixed-length buffers to prevent oracle attacks. The authorization logger has robust URL resolution. Test file is co-located. Minor issues: potential silent truncation in timingSafeEquals for very long tokens (MEDIUM), type assertion in authorization-logger (LOW), and module-level throw causing build-time failures (LOW). These do not block convergence as no CRITICAL or HIGH issues remain.

top_issues.description: The timingSafeEquals function writes strings to fixed-length buffers but does not handle the case where the string exceeds 256 bytes. Buffer.write() truncates silently, which could cause false positives for very long tokens.
The createAccessDenialPayload method uses a type assertion 'method as AuthDenialPayload["method"]' which could mask runtime type errors if an invalid method string is passed.
The module-level throw for missing INTERNAL_AUDIT_SECRET will cause Next.js build failures in environments where the env var is not set at build time (e.g., Vercel preview deployments).
